item.title: Andreessen-Backed AI Search Startup Exa Valued at $2.2 Billion
item.description: Exa Labs Inc., a San Francisco-based startup that wants to build a search engine tailor-made for the age of artificial intelligence, raised $250 million at a $2.2 billion valuation in a round led by venture firm Andreessen Horowitz.

item.title: Elliott Ramps Up Its AI Efforts With Key Hire From Blackstone
item.description: Elliott Investment Management hired former Blackstone Inc. executive Teresa Sweeney to run the roughly $80 billion hedge fund firm’s global research and data science group.

item.title: Tether Buys Out SoftBank Stake in Bitcoin Treasury Firm
item.description: Tether has bought out SoftBank Group Inc.’s ownership in the digital-asset treasury company Twenty One Capital Inc., adding to its control over the Bitcoin accumulator as it seeks to merge the firm with two other businesses.

item.title: India RBI to Inject $5 Billion Equivalent Cash Via Swap Auction
item.description: India’s central bank will inject liquidity to the equivalent of $5 billion via a swap auction, it said in a statement.

item.title: French Companies Bid for €10 Billion Europe AI Gigafactory
item.description: A consortium of European companies will bid on a €10 billion ($11.6 billion) project to build a major data center campus in France as part of the continent’s effort to boost artificial intelligence infrastructure.

item.title: India Gets First Rainfall Hedge as El Niño Threatens Monsoon
item.description: India’s largest agricultural exchange has introduced its first weather derivatives, allowing investors to hedge against monsoon-related risks.

item.title: Video Adoption Propels Global Podcast Sales to Record High of $9.2 Billion
item.description: According to a new report, 73% of the podcast industry’s growth last year in the US came from video-related revenue

item.title: Europe’s Biggest Gas Market Isn’t Setting Aside Enough Winter Fuel
item.description: Germany’s vast storage sites are less than 30% full, and that’s a concern as the Iran war continues to squeeze supplies.

item.title: US Premarket Movers for May 20, 2026
item.description: S&P 500 Index futures rise 0.3% as of 7:45 a.m. in New York as investors digest a backdrop of surging rates volatility, heavily crowded semis exposure and euphoric positioning.

item.title: Value Stocks With Earnings Strength Post 3,500% Run Since 2000
item.description: Turns out, loading up on technology giants isn’t the only route to better returns. Value companies, too, stand a decent chance of trouncing the market — as long as several conditions are met.

item.title: French Parliament Approves Macron’s Pick to Run Central Bank
item.description: Emmanuel Macron’s nominee to run the Bank of France was confirmed by parliament, avoiding a political setback for the president and relieving uncertainty for the European Central Bank as it weighs interest-rate increases.

item.title: EM Currencies Rebound After Central Banks Step In to Halt Slide
item.description: Currencies in India and Indonesia edged off record lows after central banks stepped up efforts to stem the losses, while swaps priced a South African interest-rate hike, reflecting the continued heavy pressure on emerging markets.

item.title: India’s Treasury-Bill Yields Surge on Rate-Hike Expectation
item.description: India’s treasury bill yields surged at a weekly auction, signaling fixed-income traders are starting to price in potential interest-rate hikes by the central bank.

item.title: UBS Chief Strategist Sees US Consumer Slowdown Threatening Stock Rally
item.description: The US consumer will slow down as real disposable income growth nears zero and fiscal support fades, threatening equity markets despite strong first-quarter earnings driven by artificial intelligence, according to Bhanu Baweja, chief strategist at UBS Group AG.

item.title: Major Chatbots Found Unreliable on Elections, News Topics
item.description: Startup’s survey shows chatbots generally provide inaccurate answers when asked about news

item.title: Why It Might Take a Slowdown to Undo Hormuz Closure’s Economic Damage
item.description: As the Hormuz blockage nears the three-month mark, an inflation tipping point has been reached.

item.title: Chinese EV Maker BYD Extends Global Reach With a Small Shipping Fleet
item.description: Born of necessity during Covid, the vessels now help BYD control its supply chain amid geopolitical turmoil

item.title: US Mortgage Rates Rose to Almost Two-Month High in Latest Week
item.description: US mortgage rates increased last week to an almost two-month high, weighing on home purchase activity.

item.title: Chatbots Struggle With News Accuracy and Sourcing Ahead of Midterms
item.description: Four major artificial intelligence chatbots — OpenAI’s ChatGPT, Alphabet Inc.’s Google Gemini, Anthropic’s Claude and xAI’s Grok — are struggling to fairly and accurately answer questions about elections and geopolitics, according to a new study from Forum AI.

item.title: AI’s Promises Are Starting to Materialize – With a Human Cost
item.description: Markets are being built around it, powered by it, and priced because of it. But AI comes with a hefty human toll.

item.title: David Zahn Called the Gilt Rout. Now He’s Looking to Buy at 6%
item.description: David Zahn is looking vindicated. He’s been shunning UK bonds for more than a year, when other fund managers were piling in after selloffs.

item.title: China Cuts Spending the Most in Six Months in Blow to Economy
item.description: China scaled back government spending at its fastest pace in six months in April, a move that contributed to an unexpected slowdown in the economy across the board.

item.title: Modi’s Toffee Gift to Meloni Ignites Rally in Wrong Indian Stock
item.description: A packet of caramel toffees gifted by Indian Prime Minister Narendra Modi to his Italian counterpart sent shares of a tiny software company soaring on Wednesday.

item.title: Tech Bulls Are Looking to Nvidia for Stocks Revival
item.description: Rising bond yields have been weighing on chip stocks.

item.title: France’s Moulin Backs Shared Euro Debt for New Projects
item.description: Emmanuel Macron’s nominee to run the Bank of France said he’s in favor of more joint debt issuance in the euro area for investment in the bloc’s new economic priorities.

item.title: Advocacy Groups Seek FTC Probe Into Roblox for Kid Spending
item.description: The National Center on Sexual Exploitation and Fairplay, two advocacy groups focused on child safety, are asking the US Federal Trade Commission to investigate Roblox Corp.’s game mechanics, which they claim coerce kids into spending unreasonable amounts of money on the platform.

item.title: UK House-Price Growth Grinds to a Halt After Loan Costs Surge
item.description: UK house prices failed to grow over the past year as headwinds from rising mortgage rates, political uncertainty and the Iran war build.

item.title: Tankmaker Rheinmetall Preps First Public Bond Deal Since 2010
item.description: Rheinmetall AG is preparing its first public bond sale in nearly 16 years as Europe’s defense industry booms.

item.title: Rice Prices Extend Rally to One-Year High as Crop Concerns Mount
item.description: A benchmark Asia rice price rose to the highest in more than a year, as worries loom over harvests across the region.

item.title: Russia and China Agree to Expand Key Cross-Border Rail Gateway
item.description: Russia and China signed an agreement on the joint construction of a new railway link across their shared border, part of a broader push to expand transport ties and trade between the two countries.
